title: QA: execute_bash mixes cfg branches sync/async output handling
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/tools/bash/mod.rs:319-401
## Violation
`execute_bash` is 83 lines, branches on `run_in_background`, then on `#[cfg(windows)]`, constructs output, truncates, sets `is_error`. SRP violation; cyclomatic ~12.

Quoted abridged:
```
pub fn execute_bash(args: &HashMap<String, Value>) -> (String, bool) {
    let Some(command) = args.get("command")... else { ... };
    if let Err(msg) = validate_command(command) { ... }
    let run_in_background = args.get("run_in_background")...
    if run_in_background { ... } else {
        #[cfg(windows)] let output = { ... };
        #[cfg(not(windows))] let output = { ... };
```
## Mandated refactor
1. Split: `parse_bash_args`, `spawn_sync`, `spawn_async`, `render_output`.
2. Move OS-specific spawn into `platform::spawn`.
3. Name the 50_000 truncate threshold.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
